title: Russia/Ukraine deep dive: escalation, risk factors, financial fallout, exit ramps and more
description:
0:00 David Sacks: Man for the people

0:50 Escalating tensions over Russia/Ukraine; nuclear scare; evolving forms of warfare

16:27 Assessing impact of economic sanctions: first, second and third-order effects; exit ramps for Putin and Russia

34:23 Reflecting on if taking NATO expansion off the table would have prevented war; Putin's risk of ruin; lessons learned so far

46:12 New version of economic warfare; possibility of a regime change; comparisons to the Cuban Missile Crisis; realist vs. idealist; lessons for Taiwan

1:05:22 Market impact, increased volatility, thinking in decades, managing risk

1:19:22 Car T-cell therapy breakthroughs and approvals; CRISPR patent controversy; Sacks makes a final point

analysis.summary: The All-In Podcast discusses the escalating tensions between Russia and Ukraine, including the impact of economic sanctions, potential exit ramps for Putin, and the lessons learned so far. They also touch on the market impact, increased volatility, and the importance of thinking in decades when managing risk. Additionally, they cover breakthroughs in Car T-cell therapy and the CRISPR patent controversy.

takeaways.text: Economic sanctions have first, second, and third-order effects
NATO expansion may have contributed to the war
Putin's risk of ruin is high
New forms of economic warfare are emerging
Thinking in decades is crucial for managing risk
Car T-cell therapy has shown promise in treating multiple myeloma

sentiment.notes: The hosts are cautious about the ongoing conflict and its potential consequences, but also highlight the importance of thinking in decades and managing risk.

notable_moments.description: Comparison to the Cuban Missile Crisis
Discussion on the market impact of the war
